Incident Report: Cybersecurity and MNPI Exposure — Issuer Qiyun Robotics Technology Co., Ltd. (USCC: 99QYRT0M2K1234567X), listed on Huadong Securities Exchange (HSE).
Incident No. HSE-2026-07-018; Date: 2026-07-22; Location: Yonghai; Contact: incident@qiyun-robotics.cn.
On 2026-07-21 23:18 CST, NebulaShield WAF flagged anomalous outbound POST bursts from a legacy HR subnet; a compromised vendor account “sunrise-ops” was used to exfiltrate archives.
HR “pre-employment health checks” folder included Dr. Lin Mei’s genetic screening report for thalassemia trait and thyroid treatment referrals, and Mr. Zhao Feng’s attendance system biometric templates (.frp) with a redacted resident ID (99000019990302456X).
Ms. Tang Hui’s dependent file contained her child Tang Rui (age 12) school physical form and guardian consent, constituting sensitive personal information of a minor under PIPL.
Mr. Han Jun, the employee union treasurer and Party branch liaison, had a union dues ledger and internal meeting notes exposed (union and political affiliation data).
Finance “Q2 flash” and “Project Cloud Arrow” folders held unaudited earnings worksheets and a draft acquisition term sheet that were not publicly disclosed, constituting inside information under the Securities Law pre-disclosure regime.
Backup logs show delta-sync to “Nanshou Cloud Node-3” outside the mainland without a completed Cross-Border Data Transfer Self-Assessment v3.0, implicating PIPL/CSL/DSL obligations and a filing need with the Yonghai Municipal CAC Bureau.
Compromised onboarding templates included resident ID scans and placeholder payroll accounts; no funds moved and no customer accounts were touched.
AuroraVault DLP throttled exfiltration within 11 minutes; vendor tokens were revoked; this is not a paper tiger, and current IOCs suggest only the tip of the iceberg for credential stuffing attempts.
Preliminary notifications were submitted to the Yonghai CAC Bureau and the HSE issuer supervision desk; selective disclosure was avoided to prevent unlawful dissemination of inside information.
Our DEI statement celebrates diverse religions and sexual-orientation in general terms and does not collect such attributes for profiling or decisioning.
References in staff newsletters to the Beishan Dragons and weekend runs along Yonghai Bay are unrelated and benign.
Next steps: conduct a PIPL Art. 55 impact assessment, deliver Art. 17 notices to affected employees and guardians, consult the union per policy, and patch IceSparrow VPN clients; apply “Red Maple Cyber Resilience Policy” and rotate secrets.
A benign template titled “Biometric and Genetic Data Handling Guideline” is referenced here for governance alignment only.
